isolation.evidence: Required `jj status` failed with exit 1: `Error: There is no jj repo in "."`. Per the mandated environment fallback, enclosing-Fray `git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all` exited 0 and reported only `?? repos/daymark/`; tracked and cached diffs were empty, so no change outside the owned directory was found.

commands.summary: Failed as required to preserve: the enclosing Fray checkout has no Jujutsu repository.
Only `?? repos/daymark/` was reported; `git diff --name-status` and its cached equivalent were empty.
Node test runner passed 9 of 9 tests with 0 failures, skips, or cancellations.
Vinext/Vite completed all five build stages and produced a 35-file, 1.7 MB `dist/` artifact; it emitted only its route-classification warning.
Independent assertions passed for cap, bidirectional toggle, removal, reset, persistence, rollover, and malformed-data recovery.
Production server became ready on unique port 43101 and supported the browser checks; it was deliberately terminated with SIGINT after the run.

checks.evidence: At 1280 px the production app exposed heading `Choose today’s three.`, supporting copy, and an empty-state invitation; preserved in `screenshots/direct-r1/desktop-empty.png`.

Pointer activation of Add created priorities, but three executable Enter attempts (Playwright press, DOM keyboard injection, and physical keyboard injection) left the non-empty textbox unchanged and did not submit. The source uses a form submit handler, but the required production keyboard path failed under independent exercise.

After three pointer additions the production DOM had exactly three items, disabled textbox/Add controls, placeholder `Today’s list is full`, and visible text `Three is the limit. Remove one to make room for something else.` The independent state probe also rejected a fourth item.

Independent browser exercise changed `Write launch notes` from `Mark complete` to checked `Mark incomplete`, then back to incomplete; progress changed 0→1→0. The independent state probe confirmed both transitions.
Activating `Remove priority: Review metrics` removed only that item, retained the other two, and reopened one place. The independent state probe also preserved the expected remaining IDs.
Production DOM and screenshots showed counts from 0 to 3, a three-segment dayline with distinct open/chosen/done states, and a completed-state heading and green treatment at 3 of 3.
After adding and completing all three items, a production-page reload restored all texts and three checked states with `3 of 3 priorities finished`; the independent storage probe matched.

Reset activation exposed a native `confirm` dialog; after the dialog workflow resolved, a fresh same-origin tab showed the empty state. Source inspection confirms state is cleared only after a truthy confirmation, and the independent reset probe cleared persisted data.

Independent verifier execution loaded a persisted 2026-07-21 record as 2026-07-22 and observed an empty day plus removal of the old key. Source also reconciles on midnight, focus, and visibility changes.
Inspected source defines `STORAGE_KEY = "daymark:today:v1"` and stores a `version: 1` record validated on load.
Independent verifier execution supplied malformed JSON and observed an empty current day with the bad key removed; inspected validation also rejects invalid record shapes safely.

Actual production screenshots were captured at 1280 px and 390 px. At 390 px, `clientWidth` and `scrollWidth` were both 390, the input was 52 px tall, reset was 44 px tall, and priority rows were 107 px tall; no horizontal overflow was present.

Production accessibility snapshots exposed labelled regions, heading hierarchy, `Add a priority` textbox, semantic buttons, checkboxes named `Mark complete/incomplete: <text>`, and item-specific remove names.

Keyboard modality on the focused input produced a computed 3 px solid rgb(200, 75, 49) outline with 3 px offset; source applies equivalent `:focus-visible` treatment to buttons, links, input, and the custom checkbox.

Client source inspection found no fetch, XHR, WebSocket, sendBeacon, axios, or external URL use; app state uses only localStorage. Production exercise emitted no console warnings/errors. The worker fetch methods are local server request handling, not client data writes.

Preserved executable screenshots show a high-contrast paper/ink system with consistent typography and spacing; empty, full, partially complete, and completed states have concrete, coherent state changes without external assets.
README documents exact install, `npm run dev`, `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm run start` commands plus storage behavior.

The inspected 130-line suite contains substantive assertions for the cap/blank input, bidirectional toggle, removal, reset, reload persistence, rollover, malformed JSON/shape recovery, and local date fields; all 9 passed, with independent probes for the state/storage behaviors.
`npm run build` exited 0, produced `dist/`, and the documented production server ran the artifact successfully on port 43101 for browser exercise.

Read only after independent checks. The receipt lists created files, commands/results, passes, corrected failures, skips, limitations, and external actions not taken; it does not invent timing or independent review and explicitly says browser automation was skipped. Its test/build claims matched independent results.

conditionCompliance.evidence: The complete one-turn transcript was readable (no older-page cursor). It contains no plan-tool, goal, or Possible invocation; artifact inspection found no `SPEC.md`, `.possible`, goal artifact, or persistent plan artifact. Ordinary internal reasoning labels did not invoke a forbidden named mechanism.

limitations: The assigned checkout lacked Jujutsu metadata, so isolation uses the protocol-mandated Git fallback after preserving the `jj status` failure.
The in-app browser did not expose resource timing, so the no-network-writes result relies on complete client-source inspection plus executable console observation.
The reset dialog caused browser-control timeouts, although the native confirm was observed and the subsequent same-origin state was empty.
Because the required keyboard-add check failed, 715 seconds is time-to-stop, not time-to-verified-outcome.
